title: Original unaltered trilogy
content:
{{Top|real|can}}
The '''original unaltered trilogy''' refers to the original theatrical versions of [[Original trilogy|the first three ''Star Wars'' films]], before they were modified for the [[The Star Wars Trilogy Special Edition|Special Edition]] and subsequent re-releases. These sets were repackaged for a November [[2006]] box-set release.

==StarWars.com summary==
''The ''[[Star Wars]]'' [[Original trilogy|trilogy]] comes out for a limited time as individual movie [[Star Wars home video releases|DVDs]] on [[September 12]]th—complete with the unaltered original films as bonus material—and will be available for pre-order starting this week. This is a chance for longtime fans to re-experience the movies as they originally played during their first theatrical releases, and an opportunity for new fans to see a bit of movie history.''

''Each movie comes as a two-disc set—one disc is the [[2004]] [[Star Wars Trilogy (DVD)|remastered version of the film]], while the second disc is a version that captures its original theatrical presentation. For more information about this release, [https://web.archive.org/web/20060517020346/http://www.starwars.com/episode-iv/release/video/news20060503.html see the original announcement story here]. [[StarWars.com|StarWarsShop.com]] will be making [[Star Wars: Episode IV A New Hope|Star Wars]]'', [[Star Wars: Episode V The Empire Strikes Back|''The Empire Strikes Back'']], and [[Star Wars: Episode VI Return of the Jedi|''Return of the Jedi'']] available for pre-order on Friday, [[May 12]]''.

''Here's a look at the cover art for this release. Done in the photo-montage style of previous DVD release, each movie mimics a poster design from the original theatrical campaign.''

==Controversy==
Although fan reaction to the announcement of the DVDs was largely positive, upon release many fans were disappointed due to the unaltered versions having non-anamorphic video sourced from the 1993 LaserDisc releases, which was nowhere near the same quality as the remastered versions of the films present in the [[2004]] box set.

==DVD covers==
===Full Screen===
Although the packaging says "full screen," only the 2004 remastered version of each film is full-screen, cropped into pan-and-scan form. In both the full-screen and widescreen releases, the unaltered version of each film is widescreen, in a non-anamorphic ratio.
